chapter_context.summary: Concludes the book with the prophecy of a time of unprecedented trouble followed by the glorious resurrection of the dead, some to everlasting life and others to shame, and the final command to Daniel to seal the book until the time of the end.

outline.text: The Time of Trouble, the Great Deliverance, and Resurrection of Dead
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Time of Trouble, the Great Deliverance, and Resurrection of Dead (12:1-4), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

outline.text: The Final Questions on Time: Sealing the Book until the End
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Final Questions on Time: Sealing the Book until the End (12:5-13), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

key_themes.title: The Sovereignty of God Over Kingdoms
key_themes.description: A central message of Daniel is that God rules in the kingdom of men, setting up and deposing kings according to His eternal purpose.

key_themes.title: Faithfulness under Pressure
key_themes.description: Daniel and his friends demonstrate unwavering allegiance to God's law in the face of compromise, death, and exile.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the faithfulness and courage demonstrated in Chapter 12, how can you stand firm in your convictions when faced with cultural and societal pressures to compromise?
In what ways does the vision of God's ultimate eternal kingdom in this chapter bring hope and stability to your daily life?

cultural_historical.title: Babylonian and Persian Courts
cultural_historical.text: Daniel was trained in the literature and language of the Chaldeans, serving as a high-ranking official under both Babylonian and Medo-Persian administrations, illustrating the historical reality of the Jewish diaspora.
